45.reason: [This loop skips non-alphabetic characters correctly, but if EOF is encountered before any alphabetic character, it returns EOF. However, after reading a word, the function does not return a value in all paths 鈥 specifically, after writing '\0', it falls through without an explicit return, leading to undefined behavior.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [This loop skips non-alphabetic characters correctly, but if EOF is encountered before any alphabetic character, it returns EOF. However, after reading a word, the function does not return a value in all paths 鈥 specifically, after writing '\0', it falls through without an explicit return, leading to undefined behavior.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [This loop skips non-alphabetic characters correctly, but if the file ends without any alphabetic character after the last word, it may not handle EOF properly in all cases. However, the bigger issue is below.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [This skips non-alphabetic characters correctly, but may cause undefined behavior when c is EOF because isalpha(EOF) is undefined]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [This loop skips non-alphabetic characters, but if the file starts with EOF, fgetc returns EOF, and isalpha(EOF) is undefined behavior because isalpha expects an unsigned char or EOF. Also, the assignment inside isalpha may cause issues with sign extension on some systems.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Calling isalpha on a char that may be EOF (which is negative) leads to undefined behavior because isalpha expects an unsigned char or EOF.]

60.reason: [When c is EOF, isalpha(EOF) is undefined behavior; also, after reading last word, loop exits without returning EOF properly]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [When c is EOF, isalpha(EOF) is undefined behavior; also, after reading last word, loop exits without returning EOF properly]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Same issue: passing possibly negative char values (if char is signed) to isalpha, which expects unsigned char or EOF. This can lead to undefined behavior. Also, after reading a non-alpha character, the loop exits but that character is not pushed back, which is okay per logic, but the handling of EOF inside isalpha is unsafe.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Same issue: passing possibly negative char (including EOF) to isalpha causes undefined behavior.]
